_comment: ubuntnix declared archive package set (SPEC.md §4.4, §6; GitHub issue #8, milestone M1). This is the committed input bin/ubx-resolve (the `ubx update` archive-pins portion) resolves against the real apt solver to produce archive.lock.json. 'packages' is exactly the flat deb-name list shape SPEC.md §6's `ubuntnix.debs = [ ... ]` primitive takes -- kept here as a plain JSON file (readable by tooling with no `nix` binary, same reasoning as archive.lock.json itself: see nix/archive.nix's header) until a real `ubuntnix.debs` module option exists to declare it from instead (M2+, once the module system has something to compose into; see bin/ubx-resolve's header for the migration note). 'components' is the enabled subset of noble's four archive components that bin/ubx-resolve's sources.list generation and validation cover -- main/universe/restricted/multiverse are all SUPPORTED by the resolver, but SPEC.md §5 defaults a machine to main+universe only, with restricted/multiverse a per-machine opt-in toggle that is real system config (M6 scope, not expressible here yet). Seeded 2026-07-20 with the exact package names already pinned in archive.lock.json (issue #7), so the first real `bin/ubx-resolve` run resolves a declared set whose closure is a superset of that lockfile's -- see bin/ubx-resolve's header for why a real apt solve necessarily pulls in more than these 5 names (their actual runtime library dependencies, which the issue #7 hand-built fixture did not attempt to enumerate).
